briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. Today's climate briefing for April 22, 2026, will now begin.

sections.title: 🔴 Climate Change: The Shadow Over Our Lives and Health
sections.content: Today's collected news once again reminded us of the severe impacts of climate change on various aspects of our lives. Domestically, climate change is leading to an increase in heatstroke, infectious diseases, and even allergic conditions [1, 14]. In the National Assembly, the climate crisis has been recognized as a 'mental health disaster,' leading to public discourse on policy shifts [17]. Internationally, warnings have emerged that climate change is already claiming lives in Europe and that the risks are increasing [68], while a report indicates that 99% of U.S. cities have warmed due to climate change since 1970 [70]. Notably, climate change is causing stress among university students [67, 76, 77], means greater suffering for pollen allergy sufferers [100], and a report that nearly half of American children are inhaling dangerous levels of air pollution [106] highlights the urgent need for immediate protective measures for vulnerable populations in our society. In California, an insurance crisis has emerged, prompting efforts to address climate change and insurance coverage issues [78], demonstrating that climate change is profoundly impacting systems across society.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Multifaceted Efforts Towards a Carbon-Neutral Society
sections.content: Amidst this crisis, various efforts are actively underway domestically to transition towards a carbon-neutral society. Several local governments, including Jangseong-gun, Ulsan Dong-gu, and Anyang-si, along with KB Financial Group, operated 'Climate Change Week,' spreading Earth Day commemorative lights-out events and carbon-neutral practice campaigns [3, 6, 7, 8, 10, 11, 15, 16, 19, 28, 34]. Notably, Siheung City established a 2030 environmental education roadmap to fully implement its 'Carbon-Neutral City' initiative [18], and in Pyeongtaek City, the 2050 Carbon Neutral Green Growth Committee was officially launched [39]. In the industrial sector, the Korea Technology Finance Corporation (KODIT) is fostering leading carbon-neutral companies through 'Net Zero Members' [20, 42, 44], and corporate contributions are also continuing, such as promoting the establishment of carbon-neutral industrial complexes through a pre-financing commitment model via 'THE CREATIVE 2026' project [21, 40]. Furthermore, Sinan-gun is accelerating its response to climate change in the aquaculture industry [5, 9], and South Korea and Vietnam are strengthening cooperation on climate change response, signing comprehensive agreements including power and water management [12]. Efforts are thus being made in various ways at national, regional, and industrial levels.

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and the Challenge of Increasing Power Demand
sections.content: Accelerating the transition to renewable energy is essential for achieving carbon neutrality goals. Prime Minister Kim mentioned the Sihwa and Hwaong areas as optimal locations for supplying power to the Seoul metropolitan area through renewable energy, pushing for large-scale solar power generation and emphasizing the need for rapid progress in renewable energy transition [46, 50, 52, 55, 57, 60, 61, 62]. However, with domestic power demand projected to reach up to 138 GW by 2040, an increase of 7-11% from previous forecasts [56, 63], warnings have emerged that innovation in the distribution grid is urgently needed alongside the expansion of renewable energy production [59]. KAIST researchers also pointed out that South Korea must reduce its reliance on LNG and accelerate a qualitative transition to renewable energy [54]. The industrial sector has set a '100% renewable energy' target and begun supporting energy facilities for small and medium-sized enterprises [58], and the direct procurement methods of renewable energy by IT companies are also noteworthy [48]. Internationally, the possibility of a 'gyro generator' utilizing wave energy in Japan changing the energy landscape has been raised [51], and the EU has announced plans to provide incentives for electricity bill reductions and phasing out fossil fuel vehicles to protect households amidst the energy crisis caused by the war in Iran [105].

sections.title: 🌍 Global Climate Change Awareness and Response: Challenges and New Approaches
sections.content: The international community is raising awareness and seeking solutions for climate change, while simultaneously facing several challenges. A report was released, stating that temperatures in South Dakota have risen by 2 degrees and urging leaders to take action [66]. NATO also published a report on the security implications of climate change, emphasizing the comprehensive nature of the climate crisis [79]. Meanwhile, news that the COP 31 presidency is prioritizing the global economy despite worsening climate impacts [88], and a bill protecting fossil fuel companies from climate and greenhouse gas-related lawsuits awaiting signature [86], suggest difficulties in achieving international consensus and implementation for climate action. Nevertheless, the 2026 IAF Global Space Climate Change Conference is scheduled to be held in Kigali, Rwanda, marking the first time in Africa [72], and climate awareness is gradually spreading, with 58% of people responding that climate change is real and humans are responsible [99]. Notably, reports that humor and positive approaches are emerging in climate change response instead of doomerism [87, 94] demonstrate the potential for new approaches to make climate action more appealing [69].

briefing.closing: Through today's briefing, we have examined the multifaceted impacts of climate change on our lives and society, as well as domestic and international efforts to address them. Overcoming the climate crisis requires more swift and comprehensive action based on scientific foundations. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: The initial phase of establishing the 12th Basic Plan for Long-term Electricity Supply and Demand (Jeongibon), which forms the foundation for energy policy formulation, has been unveiled. Predictions were made using base and upward scenarios, considering factors such as the spread of AI usage and the speed of carbon neutrality implementation. The peak electricity demand required by 2040 is projected to increase by up to 1.4 times from the current approximately 100GW, reaching 131.8GW, and potentially as high as 138.2GW.

summary.cause: The spread of Artificial Intelligence (AI) usage and the speed of carbon neutrality implementation were cited as key factors contributing to the increase in electricity demand.
summary.outlook: This prediction will serve as a foundation for future energy policy formulation.
